title: Grammitis phlegmaria
taxonomy: Grammitis phlegmaria belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. This classification places Grammitis phlegmaria within the larger group of vascular plants, characterized by the presence of xylem and phloem tissue. As a member of the Polypodiaceae family, Grammitis phlegmaria exhibits typical characteristics of a fern, such as its leafy appearance and reproductive structures. The specific classification of Grammitis phlegmaria within the fern family is further divided into several subgroups, each with distinct features. Further research is needed to determine the exact taxonomic classification of Grammitis phlegmaria within the fern family.
uses: Grammitis phlegmaria, also known as the silver fern, is a plant species native to the subtropical regions of the world.  It has been traditionally used in various forms of folk medicine for its perceived health benefits.
